arguments.content:
# Commitments Tracking System

## Overview

This workspace tracks obligations, signals, decisions, and parked ideas for executive workflow management.

## Directory Structure

```
commitments/
├── README.md           # This file
├── open/               # Active commitments requiring attention
├── resolved/           # Completed commitments
├── signals/
│   ├── pending/        # Signals awaiting triage
│   └── expired/        # Stale signals (older than 24h)
├── decisions/          # Recorded decisions with rationale
├── parked-ideas/       # Ideas saved for later review
├── intelligence/       # Informational signals routed here
└── triage-log.md       # Triage execution history
```

## Schema Reference

### Signals (in signals/pending/)

Signals are raw inputs that may become commitments. Frontmatter:

```yaml
type: signal
created_at: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
source: meeting/sarah-standup
immediacy: real-time | high | normal | low
expires_at: 2024-01-16T10:30:00Z  # 24h for executives
destination: commitments/open/ or commitments/intelligence/
confidence: 0.0-1.0  # How certain this should become a commitment
```

### Commitments (in open/ or resolved/)

Frontmatter:

```yaml
type: commitment
created_at: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
title: Brief description
status: open | waiting | in-progress | done
priority: critical | high | normal | low
delegated_to: person-name  # If someone else owns this
resolution_path: commitments/resolved/FILENAME.md  # When done
stale_after: 7  # Days before flagging as stale
last_updated: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
```

### Decisions (in decisions/)

Frontmatter:

```yaml
type: decision
created_at: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
title: What was decided
rationale: Why this decision was made
stakeholders: [person1, person2]  # Who was present/informed
impact: team | product | company  # Scope of decision
follow_up: 2024-02-15  # When to assess outcome
outcome: pending | validated | invalidated
```

### Parked Ideas (in parked-ideas/)

Frontmatter:

```yaml
type: idea
created_at: 2024-01-15T10:30:00Z
title: Brief description
context: Where this came from
priority: low  # Ideas start low
resurface_at: 2024-02-15  # When to review again
status: parked | active | discarded
```

## Workflow

### Triage (3x daily: 9am, 1pm, 6pm)

1. Check delegated items (status=waiting) — flag if not updated in 2 days
2. Check overdue items — escalate urgency
3. Expire signals older than 24h
4. Promote high-confidence signals to commitments
5. Route informational signals to intelligence/
6. Log triage summary

### Digest (morning 8am, evening 5pm, weekdays)

Group commitments by:
- DELEGATED — items waiting on others
- OWNED — items you need to act on
- DECISIONS PENDING — items needing your decision
- RECENT DECISIONS — decisions from last 7 days

## Commands

- "show commitments" — Get full digest
- "who owes me what?" — Check delegation status
- "/plan <description>" — Create structured execution plan

## Calibration

- Signal expiration: 24 hours (executives move fast)
- Delegation follow-up: Draft polite check-in after 2 days
- Most commitments are delegations, not personal tasks
- Keep communications scannable: bullets, one-liners

# Open Commitments

Track all active commitments here. Each commitment file should include:
- Title and description
- Status (pending/in-progress/blocked)
- Delegated to (if applicable)
- Due date or expiration
- Progress notes

# Resolved Commitments

Archive of completed or closed commitments. Keep for reference and pattern analysis.

# Pending Signals

Actionable items extracted from conversations, emails, and meetings. Awaiting triage and commitment creation.

# Expired Signals

Signals that were not acted upon within 24 hours. Executives move fast - stale signals are noise.

# Decisions Log

Record of key decisions made, including:
- Decision description
- Rationale
- People present
- Date
- Expected outcomes
- Follow-up required

# Parked Ideas

Idea capture for concepts that aren't ready for execution. Resurfaced periodically for review.

arguments.goal: Executive triage. Read commitments/README.md for schema. Priority order: (1) Check delegated items (status=waiting, delegated_to set) — if not updated in 2 days, flag for follow-up and draft a polite check-in message. (2) Check overdue items — escalate urgency. (3) Expire signals older than 24 hours (executives move fast, stale signals are noise). (4) For signals with immediacy=realtime, broadcast immediately via message tool. (5) Promote high-confidence signals to commitments — default resolution_path to needs_decision for ambiguous items. (6) Route informational signals to intelligence (write MemoryDoc to context/intel/). (7) Append triage summary to commitments/triage-log.md. (8) If anything needs attention, send a concise alert — executives scan, not read.
arguments.name: commitment-triage

arguments.goal: Executive commitments digest. Read commitments/README.md for schema. Gather all open commitments via memory_tree and memory_read. Group by responsibility: (1) DELEGATED — items where delegated_to is set, with days since delegation and follow-up status. (2) OWNED — items you need to act on personally, sorted by urgency. For agent_can_handle items, note what the agent would do and ask permission. (3) DECISIONS PENDING — items with resolution_path=needs_decision. (4) RECENT DECISIONS — decisions captured in the last 7 days (from commitments/decisions/), including any needing outcome assessment. Keep each item to one line. End with pending signal count and 'Did I miss anything?' Send via message tool.
arguments.name: commitment-digest

arguments.content:
# Executive Commitment Calibration

- Group commitments by responsibility type in digests — delegated items shown separately from owned items
- For delegation follow-ups, draft a polite check-in rather than a blunt status request
- Only capture explicit decisions, not brainstorming or hypotheticals ('yeah let's do X' = decision; 'maybe we should' = not a decision)
- Signal expiration is 24 hours — executives move fast, stale signals are noise
- Most CEO commitments are delegations, not personal tasks — default delegated_to when someone else is mentioned
- When capturing decisions, note who was present and what it affects — executives revisit decisions frequently
- Keep all communications scannable: bullet points, one-liners, no paragraphs
- Start conservative: surface everything, don't auto-promote signals or auto-dispatch agent_can_handle without approval
